relatives = [[Austin Buchanan]] (half-brother)
[[Jamie Jones-Buchanan]] (half-brother) }} }} '''Jodie Broughton''' (born 9 January 1988) is an English former [[rugby league]] footballer who last played on the {{rlp|wg}} for the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He is a four-time [[England Knights|England Knights international]]. He has previously been in the system of the [[Leeds Rhinos]] and spent time on loan from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Hull F.C.]] in 2008 and 2009. Broughton has also played for the [[Salford City Reds]] and the [[Huddersfield Giants]]. He has spent time on loan from the [[Huddersfield Giants|Giants]] at [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]]. He has also played for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the top flight and from the [[Catalans Dragons|Dragons]] at [[Toulouse Olympique]] and [[Halifax RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. In 2011, Broughton was named Fastest Man in the [[Super League]].{{cite web| url=http://www.reds.co.uk/rugby-news/jodie-broughton-becomes-rugby-leagues-fastest-man| title=Jodie Broughton Becomes Rugby League's Fastest Man| publisher=salford city reds rugby club| website=reds.co.uk| accessdate=12 November 2011}} ==Background== Broughton was born in [[Leeds]], West Yorkshire, England. He is the half-brother of former Leeds Rhinos teammate [[Jamie Jones-Buchanan]]. ==Early career== Broughton began playing rugby league for local sides Milford Marlins A.R.L.F.C. and Queens A.R.L.F.C. and was also selected to represent the Leeds Service Area. He has won a number of competitions including the Leeds and District Cup, Yorkshire Cup, National Cup, West Riding Cup and the Yorkshire combination league/cup. ==Club career== ===Leeds Rhinos=== Broughton signed for the [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] Academy in 2006. In the 2007 season he made 15 appearances for the Junior side scoring 10 tries.{{cite web|url=http://leedsrugby.dnsupdate.co.uk/leedsrhinos/acadprofiles/squad-page.asp?ID=193|title=Broughtons Academy Record|publisher=Leeds Rhinos|date=12 October 2007|accessdate=18 June 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081120032550/http://leedsrugby.dnsupdate.co.uk/leedsrhinos/acadprofiles/squad-page.asp?ID=193|archive-date=20 November 2008|url-status=dead}} ===Hull F.C.=== On 20 June 2008 Broughton signed for [[Hull F.C.]] on a months loan.{{cite web|url=http://www.hullfc.com/hullfc/contents/?p=1473|title=Hull Sign Broughton on Loan|publisher=hullfc.com|date=20 June 2008|accessdate=18 June 2009}}{{Dead link|date=February 2020 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }} He made his club and Super League début two days later at [[Odsal Stadium|Odsal]] in a 36–22 defeat by the [[Bradford Bulls]].{{cite web|url=http://www.hullfc.com/hullfc/contents/?p=1476|title=Bulls Down Hull|publisher=hullfc.com|date=23 June 2008|accessdate=18 June 2009}}{{Dead link|date=February 2020 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }} A week later he scored his first Super League try in a 24–22 loss to [[Warrington Wolves]]. He went on to make a total of 5 appearances for Hull F.C. over the season.{{cite web|url=http://www.hullfc.com/hullfc/contents/?p=1594|title=Hicks Denies Hull|publisher=hullfc.com|date=29 June 2008|accessdate=18 June 2009}}{{Dead link|date=February 2020 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }} Following his return to Leeds Rhinos, Broughton was again the subject of a loan move to Hull F.C. when he signed for a second stint on 16 April 2009.{{cite web|url=http://www.hullfc.com/hullfc/contents/?p=3174|title=Hull Sign Broughton Again|publisher=hullfc.com|date=16 April 2009|accessdate=18 June 2009}}{{Dead link|date=February 2020 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }} He scored a brace of tries in Hull's 37–22 defeat by [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] on 13 June 2009.{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8091974.stm|title=Wakefield Beat Hull|publisher=BBC Sport|date=13 June 2009|access-date=18 June 2009}} ===Salford Red Devils=== He joined Salford for 2010http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=16152 {{dead link |date=August 2024|fix-attempted=yes}} and has since seen his career flourish at the Greater Manchester club establishing himself in the Reds' first team and being drafted into the England training squad. ===Huddersfield Giants=== He signed for 2013 [[League Leaders Shield]] winners [[Huddersfield Giants]] in October 2013, he made his senior début on 7 March 2014 and scored a try in a 12–12 draw with the [[Leeds Rhinos]]. In March 2015 he played for the [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in their [[League 1 Cup]] game against [[Barrow Raiders]].{{cite news | last = | first = | author-link = | title = Huddersfield Giants winger Jodie Broughton benefiting from his time at Oldham | work = | place = | pages = | publisher = The Huddersfield Daily Examiner | date = 2015-03-18 | url = http://www.examiner.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/huddersfield-giants-winger-jodie-broughton-8865571 | format = | accessdate = 2015-08-21}} ===Catalans Dragons=== On 18 August 2015, Jodie signed for the [[Super League]] French franchise the [[Catalans Dragons]] on a two-year deal starting in 2016.{{cite news | last = | first = | author-link = | title = Catalans Dragons sign Jodie Broughton from Huddersfield Giants | work = | place = | pages = | publisher = Sky Sports | date = 2015-08-15 | url = http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/9955020/catalan-dragons-announce-jodie-broughton-signing | format = | accessdate = 2015-08-21}} ===Batley Bulldogs=== On 24 August 2020 it was announced that Broughton had signed for the [[Batley Bulldogs]] for the 2021 season.{{cite web|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=24 Aug 2020|accessdate=3 Jan 2021|title=Batley snap up Jodie Broughton|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/batley-snap-up-jodie-broughton/}} Broughton retired from playing rugby league at the end of the 2021 season.{{cite news|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/ex-rhinos-winger-broughton-announces-retirement-3446447 |title=Jodie Broughton retires: Batley Bulldogs' ex-Leeds Rhinos academy winger hangs up boots but will remain in rugby league |newspaper=Yorkshire Evening Post |date=5 November 2021 |access-date=20 April 2025}} ==Personal== Broughton Graduated from Salford University in 2012. He is now a Quantity Surveyor and works with his former club the Huddersfield Giants as a Positive well-being coach for their Scholarship programme. ==References== {{reflist|2}} ==External links== *[http://www.catalansdragons.com/en/fiche-329-broughton-jodie/ Catalans Dragons profile] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20081120032550/http://leedsrugby.dnsupdate.co.uk/leedsrhinos/acadprofiles/squad-page.asp?ID=193 Leeds Rhinos profile] *[http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/08/06/20/RUGBYL_Hull.html Broughton brought in] *[http://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/rugby-league-headlines/Leeds-Rhinos-Loanee-Jodie39s-out.5296685.jp Leeds Rhinos: Loanee Jodie's out to impress] * (archived by web.archive.org) [https://web.archive.org/web/20130612025400/http://hullfc.com/past-players/B Stats → PastPlayers → B at hullfc.com] * (archived by web.archive.org) [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304053419/http://www.hullfc.com/first-team/profile/190385/jodie-broughton Statistics at hullfc.com] *[https://www.superleague.co.uk/player/281/jodie%3Cbr-%3Ebroughton SL profile] {{DEFAULTSORT:Broughton, Jodie}} [[Category:1988 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:21st-century English sportsmen]] [[Category:Alumni of the University of Salford]] [[Category:Batley Bulldogs players]] [[Category:Catalans Dragons players]] [[Category:England Knights national rugby league team players]] [[Category:English people of Jamaican descent]] [[Category:English rugby league players]] [[Category:Halifax Panthers players]] [[Category:Hull F.C. players]] [[Category:Leeds Rhinos players]] [[Category:Oldham R.L.F.C. players]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Leeds]] [[Category:Rugby league wingers]] [[Category:Salford RLFC players]] [[Category:Sportspeople of Jamaican descent]] [[Category:Toulouse Olympique players]]
